caption.text: So here we are on the landing page.
And already, first of all, what I can see is that
in the hero section we understand everything right away,
the value proposition is clear, so we’re going to take care of our links
so create them, manage them,
and on top of that, we have all the features offered.
So really, for this hero section, I have almost nothing to say.
And honestly the design is really nice, I like it a lot.
We have a little preview of the SaaS, that’s really good.
And on top of that,
a very good point is having put these little bullets under the CTA.
That removes friction
and it’s really, it’s really a plus to have done that. So bravo.
So let’s go, we’re going to
start for free right now.
What I can already see is that
another very good point, haha.
Here we have a recap of the features,
so of what’s offered in the product,
that’s a very good thing.
And on top of that, we only have
two fields. So email and password. For this kind of SaaS,
you don’t need more and it reduces all the friction.
So that’s a very good thing.
So “Create my profile for free.”
Honestly, the wording is very good
from what I can see. Okay, we land on the interface
and the first thing I see,
there’s no onboarding,
we land directly and in the end it’s up to us to know what to do.
We have some stats here,
quick actions. It’s a good thing to put quick actions,
you just shouldn’t have too many either
to avoid losing the user, who might think,
“What do I start with?”
“What do I have to do?”
Especially since here we don’t have any checklist
or anything guided.
So I’m going to browse the SaaS a bit, you can directly create a link,
create a collection, I imagine that’s the projects,
and make a link in bio, so a link in bio profile.
Okay, and we have all the recent activity with
again the option to create a link.
Very good. Here we have all the links listed.
A good thing I see that’s been done
is putting a CTA
directly to create a link instead of leaving
something empty with the text
“no data” like I used to put
like I used to say in an earlier video.
So that’s a very good thing to have done.
Same for projects, very good. The contacts,
no contacts, so I imagine that’s generated via the links
when we do email capture. The profile
that will come
later. Okay. It’s good to have put a preview, it’s good because it
helps create a bit of excitement
and so keeps the user
until, until that future feature arrives. That’s good.
Choose the plan. Well, up to here,
with a “Contact us.”
Perfect. And the settings, okay, perfect.
So what I could say to increase
this notion of user experience in your SaaS
in the end, what could be good to do
would be to create a guided onboarding
in which
you immediately have them create their first link.
Actually, that’s what we call the “aha moment” there, in your SaaS
the goal is to create and manage links.
So instead of leaving them alone
to discover the SaaS,
what would be good would be
to immediately have them create their first link by guiding them,
so telling them, “Here, you’ve landed on the dashboard,
it’s time to create the first link,
the first link.” So for example, it could be with hotspots,
it could be
like a kind of tutorial where you guide them,
it can be through different means.
Or it could even be before entering the SaaS,
once the sign-up is done,
instead of showing the dashboard directly,
you show the dashboard in the background with a pop-up
to have them create their first link right away.
So here, how does it work? There’s a new link.
There. It would be to put a pop-up
or immediately have them create their first link so they get a first win
pretty quickly, and they get that
aha moment. That thing of, “Okay,
I’ve arrived, and in 2 minutes I created my first link and I already
it’s already set up.” So at that point, you have them create it directly,
upload the link,
and actually at the end
you have them add the little details.
There. So now I see—I see how it works.
It’s pretty good, the little detail
you can add a pre-view image,
that’s cool. There’s already one.
You can add a description, you can choose a project.
That
isn’t necessarily mandatory to put it at the start of the onboarding, the project,
but just for creating the link, okay.
And same for the squeeze page,
not necessarily in the onboarding either because you shouldn’t put too much,
you really need to highlight one feature.
You have them create the link there, you put confetti everywhere,
maybe not confetti, but you give micro wins to tell them,
“There, you’ve created your first link.”
and then add little hotspots, for example in the “My projects” section
so they see here: “You can
centralize your links in different projects, etc.” And actually you guide them
step by step, without necessarily making everything mandatory.
but it lets them discover the SaaS in a fun way
and at the same time brings that first win and
makes them say, “Okay, I managed to understand how it works,
it’s simple,
I know how to use it, and in 3 minutes I’ve understood the whole SaaS.”
So that’s it for the little analysis, I hope you liked it,
anyway your SaaS is really clean and I think I’m going to use it.
